_notes: PyQuiz bank for Notebook 3: Python Basics via Pseudocode & Flowcharts. Covers values, types, arithmetic, comparison/boolean, strings, and a few built-ins. Avoids conditionals, loops, and collections — those are introduced in NB4 and NB5.

questions.description: Your first function. Return the string 'Hello, World!' exactly — same capitalization, comma, and exclamation mark.
Return the sum of the two numbers. add_numbers(3, 4) should return 7.
questions.answer_code:
def add_numbers(a, b):
    return a + b

questions.description: Return double the given number. double_number(5) should return 10.
Return the area of a rectangle given its length and width. calculate_area(4, 5) should return 20.
questions.answer_code:
def calculate_area(length, width):
    return length * width

questions.description: Return the remainder when a is divided by b. Use the modulo operator (%). remainder_division(10, 3) should return 1.
questions.answer_code:
def remainder_division(a, b):
    return a % b
questions.hint: The % operator gives the remainder of integer division.

questions.description: Return the absolute value of a number, using the built-in abs() function. absolute_value(-5) should return 5.
Round the number to the nearest integer using the built-in round() function. round_number(4.7) should return 5.
Return the larger of two numbers, using the built-in max() function. max_number(5, 10) should return 10.
questions.answer_code:
def max_number(a, b):
    return max(a, b)
questions.hint: max(a, b) returns the larger one.

questions.description: Return the number of characters in the string, using the built-in len() function. string_length('hello') should return 5.
Return the two names joined with a single space between them. combine_strings('Ada', 'Lovelace') should return 'Ada Lovelace'.
questions.answer_code:
def combine_strings(first_name, last_name):
    return first_name + ' ' + last_name
questions.hint: Concatenate with + and include a space in the middle.

questions.description: Return the string repeated the given number of times. repeat_string('ab', 3) should return 'ababab'. In Python, multiplying a string by an integer repeats it: 'a' * 3 is 'aaa'.
questions.answer_code:
def repeat_string(text, times):
    return text * times
questions.hint: Use * between the string and the number.

questions.description: Return the string 'Welcome to Earth, NAME!' where NAME is the input. alien_greeting('Spock') should return 'Welcome to Earth, Spock!'.
questions.answer_code:
def alien_greeting(name):
    return f'Welcome to Earth, {name}!'
questions.hint: Use an f-string: f'Welcome to Earth, {name}!'.

questions.description: Convert a Fahrenheit temperature to Celsius using the formula C = (F - 32) * 5/9. convert_to_celsius(32) should return 0.
questions.hint: Watch operator precedence — subtract first, then multiply by 5/9.

questions.description: Return the tip amount given a bill and a percentage (as a whole number — 15 means 15%). calculate_tip(50, 15) should return 7.5.
questions.hint: Divide the percentage by 100 to turn it into a decimal, then multiply by the bill.

questions.description: Return Body Mass Index given weight (kg) and height (m). Formula: BMI = weight / (height * height). calculate_bmi(70, 1.75) returns about 22.86.
questions.answer_code:
def calculate_bmi(weight_kg, height_m):
    return weight_kg / (height_m * height_m)
questions.hint: Divide weight by height squared. Parentheses matter.

questions.description: Return True if the number is greater than zero, otherwise False. is_positive(5) returns True; is_positive(-3) returns False. (You can return a comparison directly — no `if` needed.)
questions.hint: `number > 0` is itself a Boolean. Just return it.

questions.description: Return True if the number is even, otherwise False. Hint: a number is even when its remainder mod 2 is 0.
